description_long: This is a mid-rise office building with a rectangular footprint and a modern, utilitarian facade. The exterior is clad in light-colored concrete or stone panels, interspersed with vertical rows of windows that are evenly spaced and uniform in size. A central vertical section features a glass-enclosed stairwell or elevator shaft, adding visual interest and breaking up the otherwise repetitive window pattern. The building has at least nine visible floors, with the lower levels featuring larger window panels and what appears to be a main entrance. There is some visible signage on the facade, suggesting commercial or corporate occupancy. The overall design is functional, with minimal ornamentation, characteristic of late 20th-century urban office architecture.
description: A modern, rectangular office building with light-colored cladding, uniform vertical window rows, and a central glass stairwell or elevator shaft. The facade is functional and minimally ornamented, with visible signage and a prominent main entrance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with vertical window rows, a central glass shaft, and visible signage near the entrance.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the building's depth and the uniformity of the window pattern along the longer facade.
description_view_2: The rear oblique view reveals the glass shaft running up the building and the regular window arrangement, with some rooftop details visible.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the repetitive window pattern and the vertical division created by the central shaft.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular vertical rows of rectangular windows, evenly spaced and repeated across all floors, with a central glass shaft breaking the pattern.
